ctxMarket: Attention is centered on Solana’s expanding role in real-world asset (RWA) lending and ongoing security incidents affecting multiple blockchains. Institutional interest in Solana is rising, while security risks remain a prominent topic.
ctxTalking:
• Solana surpassing Ethereum in RWA lending and attracting institutional flows
• High-profile security breaches, including AI-enabled social engineering and fake apps targeting Solana users
• Speculation around new crypto products from X, with possible Solana connections
ctxMatters: Solana’s momentum in RWA lending signals growing adoption beyond trading. Security breaches highlight persistent risks for users and platforms, especially as attacks become more sophisticated. New product speculation from major tech players could influence future Solana integrations or user flows.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Solana’s lead in RWA lending and increased institutional participation; ongoing sophisticated security threats.
Noise: Unconfirmed speculation about X’s crypto product and its potential Solana involvement.
ctxGlossary:
RWA lending — Using blockchain to facilitate loans backed by real-world assets like property or vehicles.
Social engineering — Manipulating people into revealing confidential information, often used in cyberattacks.

readMore.title: North Korean hackers used AI-enabled social engineering in Zerion attack
Solana flips Ethereum in RWA lending as institutional flows rise in Q1
Fake Ledger app on Apple App Store linked to $9.5M crypto theft across Bitcoin, Tron, Solana
